context: Placeholder on the closed army dropdown, before one is chosen.
Placeholder on the closed character-target dropdown, before one is chosen.
Button that opens the leader comparison list for an action.
Tooltip on the choose-leader button. {free} is how many house members could take the job on now, {total} how many there are.
english: [Compare everyone in your house for this job. {free} of {total} could take it on now.]

context: Placeholder on the closed organisation-target dropdown, before one is chosen.
Placeholder on the closed province-target dropdown, before one is chosen.
Placeholder on the closed ship dropdown, before one is chosen.
Button that issues the composed action to the simulation.
Shown in place of the forecast while the action still has empty slots.
english: [Choose the remaining details to see the forecast.]

context: Heading above the context-sensitive job buttons under a selection.
The chosen leader and their odds, beside the led-by label. {leader} is a character name, {chance} a percentage.
Reason shown when an action's leader cannot be chosen, because a force is led by whoever commands it.
Wraps the reason a leader is fixed, shown beside the led-by slot. {reason} is that reason.
Label before whoever would lead the action being composed.
Shown in place of the army dropdown when the player commands none.
english: [You command no armies. Muster the levies first.]

context: Shown beside the led-by label before anyone has been chosen.
Shown in place of the ship dropdown when the player has no docked ships.
Shown at the ship slot when the chosen ship has no captain to carry the order. The ship's own name is already on screen beside it.
english: [This ship has no captain. A ship is ordered by the officer who commands it — assign one first.]

context: Fallback shown when a character cannot take on work and no more specific reason is known.
What kind of body this is: a moon orbiting a planet.
What kind of body this is: a planet.
What kind of body this is: an artificial station rather than a natural body.
One of a job's costs, listed in its tooltip. I abbreviates influence. {amount} is a number.
One of a job's costs, listed in its tooltip. M abbreviates manpower. {amount} is a number.
One of a job's costs, listed in its tooltip. S abbreviates supplies. {amount} is a number.
One of a job's costs, listed in its tooltip. W abbreviates wealth. {amount} is a number.
The bottom edge of the screen, named inside sentences like "Move to the bottom." Lower case on purpose.
The left edge of the screen, named inside sentences like "Move to the left." Lower case on purpose.
The right edge of the screen, named inside sentences like "Move to the right." Lower case on purpose.
Shown under a forecast when the job cannot be ordered at all. {reason} is the simulation's own account of why.
The skill contest behind a job's odds. {skill} is a skill name, {value} the leader's rating, {difficulty} the job's, {effect} a signed number.
Tooltip on the skill contest line, explaining how advantage moves the odds.
english: [The leader's governing skill against the job's authored difficulty. Each point of advantage shifts weight out of the bad outcomes and into the good ones; each point of deficit does the reverse.]

context: Line in a forecast listing what ordering the job costs immediately. {costs} is a list of amounts.
Tooltip on the forecast cost line, making clear the cost is paid whatever happens.
english: [Taken from your house's stores the moment the job begins. This is spent whatever the outcome — a guaranteed cost, not a risk.]

context: Line in a forecast for the days before the order even reaches its leader. {days} is a number; the d abbreviates days.
Tooltip on the order-delay line, explaining that orders travel.
english: [Your order has to physically reach the leader first. Distance from your head, and any travel in progress, add this delay before the job even starts.]

context: Line in a forecast giving how long the job takes once begun. {days} is a number.
Tooltip on the duration line.
english: [Days from the order taking effect until it resolves. A march takes at least as long as the army needs to reach its objective.]

context: Line warning that a military job is settled in the field after its roll. {operation} is the operation's name.
Tooltip explaining that the field contest is deliberately outside the percentages.
english: [These chances cover the order itself. Even a successful order is then decided by the operation — the strength, supply and order of the forces present settle it, and that contest is deliberately not folded into the percentages above.]

context: Heading above the outcome distribution in a forecast.
Tooltip on the outcome heading.
english: [The exact outcome distribution the simulation would roll against today. It moves with the leader you choose and their skill.]

context: Reserved: an outcome row's label with no annotation. {result} is the outcome name.
An outcome row for a result that opens a popup asking the player to choose. {result} is the outcome name.
Tooltip on an outcome with no authored consequence written for it.
A personal risk to the job's leader. {risk} is the risk's name; {on_failure} and {on_disaster} are percentages.
english: [{risk} risk — {on_failure} on a failure, {on_disaster} on a disaster]

context: Tooltip on a personal risk line, making clear it is conditional on a bad outcome.
english: [A personal consequence for the leader, rolled only if the job goes badly. It is conditional on the outcome above, not an additional chance on the order itself.]

context: Part of a character's hover summary, for a living character. {years} is a whole number.
A character's hover summary. Cmd, Dip, Int and Ste abbreviate the four skills, kept short because this sits under the cursor.
Part of a character's hover summary, for a dead character. {date} is a campaign date.
Shown in a house's hover summary when it currently has no head.
Shown in a character's hover summary when they belong to no house.
A house's hover summary. {titles} is how many titles it holds.
The stores line of a house's hover summary. W, M, S and I abbreviate wealth, manpower, supplies and influence; influence is shown against legitimacy.
A house's standing, in its hover summary: one of the great houses.
An organisation's standing, in its hover summary: the imperial government.
A house's standing, in its hover summary: answering to nobody.
A house's standing when it is a vassal but its liege is unknown.
A house's standing, in its hover summary. {liege} is the house it answers to.
Shown in the top-bar identity when the player's house currently has no head.
Shown in the top-bar identity when the player has no house at all.
Button in the inspector that opens the selected body's surface map.
Row label in the body inspector, counting the provinces on that body.
Row label in the body inspector, for the body's radius.
Value beside the radius row. {km} is a whole number of kilometres.
Row label in the body inspector, for the body's internal stable ID. A debugging aid shown to the player.
Line in the character inspector for a living character. {years} is a whole number.
Line in the character inspector naming where the character currently is. {place} is a province or other location name.
Line in the character inspector for a dead character. {date} is a campaign date.
Line in the character inspector giving the opinion the player's head of house holds of this character. {opinion} is a signed number such as +40.
Line in the character inspector giving this character's opinion of the player's head of house. {opinion} is a signed number.
Row label in the character inspector, before a parent's name.
Row label in the character inspector, before a spouse's name.
Line listing a character's traits. {traits} is a comma-separated list.
Placeholder text on the closed travel dropdown in the character inspector.
Shown in the inspector panel when the player has selected nothing.
english: [Select a body, province, house, or character.]

context: Marker shown on a house that no longer exists. Upper case for emphasis.
Line in the house inspector for a house of the highest tier.
Row label in the house inspector, before the name of its head.
Line in the organisation inspector for the imperial government rather than a house.
Line in the house inspector for a house that answers to nobody.
Row label in the house inspector, before the list of its living members.
Shown in place of a name when a house currently has no head.
Shown in place of a name when a vassal house's liege is unknown. An em dash.
Row label in the house inspector, before its standing obligations.
Tooltip on the obligations row, explaining that obligations are facts rather than feelings.
english: [Favours, promises and grievances binding this house. These are political facts, separate from how anyone feels: a house may dislike its creditor and still owe it.]

context: Line in the house inspector counting the titles it holds. {count} is a whole number.
Row label in the house inspector, before the name of the house it answers to.
Line for one army standing in the selected province. The crossed-swords glyph opens it. {army} is the army's name, {men} its strength.
Row label in the province inspector, before the armies and ships standing there.
Row label in the province inspector, before the name of whoever holds it.
Row label in the province inspector, for its latitude on the body.
Row label in the province inspector, for its longitude on the body.
Prefix before what the active map mode says about the selected province. {mode} is the map mode's name.
Shown in the held-by row when nobody holds the province.
Line under a province's name naming the body it sits on. {body} is a body name.
Row label in the province inspector, before its order percentage.
Tooltip on the order reading, explaining what provincial order does.
english: [How governable this province is. It scales what the province pays and how reliably it is defended, and a province left in unrest will throw off its ruler entirely.]

context: Row label in the province inspector, before its monthly output figures.
The three monthly output figures. W, M and S abbreviate wealth, manpower and supplies.
Line for one ship docked at the selected province. The anchor glyph opens it. {ship} is the ship's name.
Warning line in the province inspector when the province is counting down to revolt. {days} is a number of days.
Tooltip on the unrest warning, naming the ways out of it.
english: [Garrison it, go there in person, or hold court to restore order before the province is lost.]

context: Row label in the character inspector for the command skill, which governs warfare.
Row label in the character inspector for the diplomacy skill.
Row label in the character inspector for the intrigue skill.
Row label in the character inspector for the stewardship skill, which governs administration.
Stand-in shown where a name should be but none could be resolved.
Line in a job's tooltip listing what it costs. {costs} is a comma-separated list of amounts.
Line in a job's tooltip listing what its leader risks. {risks} is a comma-separated list.
Button that calls off a job under way.
Shown in the active-jobs panel when nothing is under way.
One row of the active-jobs panel. {job} is the job's title, {leader} who leads it, {days} how many days remain; the d abbreviates days.
Shown in the map ledger panel when no body's map is open, so there are no colours to explain.
english: [Open a body's map to see what its colours mean.]

context: Footnote in the map ledger for graded map modes, telling the player the numbers are drawn on the globe itself.
Map legend key, claim-pressure mode: a house holding some but not the most provinces on this body.
Map legend key, claim-pressure mode: the house currently leading the claim to this body.
Map legend key, claim-pressure mode: a house with too few provinces to matter to the claim.
Map legend key, military mode: no troops standing in the province.
Map legend key, military mode: troops belonging to someone other than the player.
Map legend key, military mode: the player's own troops.
Map legend key, my-realm mode: provinces the player's house holds itself.
Map legend key, my-realm mode: provinces two or more steps down the chain of vassalage.
Map legend key, my-realm mode: provinces outside the player's realm entirely.
Map legend key, my-realm mode: provinces held one step down the chain of vassalage.
Map legend key, order mode: the highest band of provincial order.
Map legend key, order mode: low order, not yet in unrest.
Map legend key, order mode: comfortable provincial order.
Map legend key, order mode: the province is in open unrest and counting down to revolt.
Map legend key, relations mode: the holder thinks well of the player's house.
Map legend key, relations mode: the holder thinks badly of the player's house.
Map legend key, relations mode: the holder has no strong opinion of the player's house.
Map legend key, relations mode: the player's own holdings, which have no opinion to show.
Map legend key, wealth mode: the middle of the range of provincial output.
Map legend key, wealth mode: the lowest provincial output.
Map legend key, wealth mode: the highest provincial output.
Heading above the list of celestial bodies in the listing panel.
The standing order of an army holding ground and giving battle if attacked.
Button that dissolves an army back into the population.
Heading above the player's armies and ships in the listing panel.
The standing order of an army holding its position without seeking battle.
Heading above the list of houses in the listing panel.
Placeholder on the closed dropdown that sends a ship somewhere.
Heading above the list of provinces in the listing panel.
Where a character is when travelling. {place} is the destination province, {date} when they arrive.
Shown where a character's location should be but none is recorded.
Stand-in for a destination province whose name could not be resolved. An ellipsis.

Tooltip on a log entry that can be clicked to select what it is about.
Toggle in the log panel that hides entries not concerning the player's house.
Tooltip on the mine-only toggle.

context: Shown in the log panel when the filter excludes every entry.
Hover description of the claim-pressure map mode, on its toolbar button.
english: [Each province's weight in the race for the Paramountcy: who leads, who is contesting, and who is out of it.]

context: Province hover hint, claim-pressure mode. {holder} is a house name, {held} how many provinces it holds on this body, {total} how many there are.
As ui.map-mode.claim.hint.held, but this house is currently leading the claim to the body.
english: [{holder} holds {held} of {total} provinces here — currently leading the claim]

context: Name of the great-house map mode, on its toolbar button.
Hover description of the holder map mode.
Province hover hint in the my-realm map mode, when the player's own house holds the province directly.
Province hover hint in the holder and great-house map modes. {holder} is a house or organisation name.
Province hover hint in the holder map mode, when nobody holds the province — it has revolted or was never taken.
Province hover hint in the my-realm map mode, when the province is held outside the player's chain of vassalage. {holder} is a house or organisation name.
Province hover hint in the my-realm map mode, when nobody holds the province.
Province hover hint when the province is held one step down the player's chain of vassalage. {holder} is a house or organisation name.

context: Plural form of ui.map-mode.holder.hint.vassal.one; {hops} is 2 or more.

context: Name of the holder map mode, on its toolbar button. Kept to one word.
Value printed on the province in the my-realm map mode when the player holds it directly. Kept short — it is drawn on the globe.
Value printed on the province in the my-realm map mode when a vassal holds it. {holder} is a house name. Kept short — it is drawn on the globe.
Hover description of the military map mode.
Province hover hint, military mode, when no troops are standing in the province.
Province hover hint, military mode. {men} is a troop count, {owner} the house whose force is strongest there.
Name of the military map mode, on its toolbar button. Kept to one word.
Hover description of the my-realm map mode.

context: Name of the my-realm map mode, on its toolbar button.
Hover description of the order map mode.
english: [How governable each province is. Provinces in unrest are marked, and will throw off their ruler if left.]

context: Province hover hint, order mode. {percent} is provincial order as a percentage; {pressure} is a phrase describing what is pushing on it.
Second line of the order-mode hover hint, shown only when the province is in unrest and counting down. {days} is a number of days.
Name of the order map mode, on its toolbar button. Kept to one word.
Hover description of the relations map mode.

context: Province hover hint, relations mode. {holder} is a house name; {opinion} is a signed number such as +40 or -15.

Province hover hint, relations mode, for the player's own holdings.

context: Name of the wealth map mode, on its toolbar button. Kept to one word.
A military operation: investing a province to take its title.
A military operation: closing a province's approaches with a ship.
A military operation: marching an army to another province.
A military operation: standing guard where the army already is.
A military operation: striking a province for loot rather than to hold it.
A military operation: refilling an army's supply train from the house's stores.
The name of a favour — an obligation one house owes another for a good turn done.

Shown before the name of a house the selected house owes a favour to.
The name of a grievance — a wrong one house holds against another.

Shown before the name of a house the selected house holds a grievance against.

Line in an obligation's tooltip naming what created it. {origin} is a short phrase.
The name of a promise — an undertaking one house has given another.
Shown before the name of a house that has promised the selected house something.
Shown before the name of a house the selected house has promised something to.
Line in an obligation's tooltip for one with no expiry date.
Line in an obligation's tooltip giving how much it counts for. {weight} is a number.
Line in a panel toggle's tooltip when that panel is currently put away.
Line in every panel toggle's tooltip spelling out the right-click affordance, which is otherwise invisible.
english: [Click to dock left, right-click to dock right.]

Tooltip on the ✕ button in every panel header.
Tooltip for the inspector panel, on its header and its toolbar toggle.

Tooltip for the active-jobs panel.

english: [What the colours on the map mean.]

context: Title of the map ledger panel, in its header.
Tooltip for the listing panel.
english: [Bodies, houses, and the forces you command.]

Tooltip for the log panel.
Title of the log panel, in its header.

One candidate row in the leader picker. {name} is a character name, {skill} their rating in the governing skill, {chance} their odds as a percentage.
Appended to a candidate row when they already hold a standing command. {assignment} names it.
Divider label in the leader picker, above house members who cannot take the job on.

One row for a house member who cannot take the job on. {name} is their name, {reason} where they are instead.
Shown in the leader picker when everyone capable is already busy.

english: [Nobody in your house can lead this.]

The influence reading in the resource bar, shown against the house's legitimacy ceiling. I abbreviates influence.

A job outcome: better than success.

Tag beside a search result naming what kind of thing it is.
